person.name: Thomas Ballard
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
On the death of his father and providing that he was of full age, Thomas inherited half of the estate comprised of the four manors of Westcombe, Spittlecombe, Sappington & Horton along with all of their lands, tenements, fees & advowsons. Perhaps the fact that Gregory’s will was not probated until 10th March 1417 is because Thomas was under age and it had not been necessary to prove the will until then. In any event he did not receive the remainder until after his mother Katherine's death. Thomas most likely had been part of the household of a knight, one of Gergory’s patrons or friends, from the age of 7 or 8, as was common at that time, and trained as a man at arms although we have no record of him having any military service. There can be little doubt that Thomas would have lived well off of the substantial income that his estates provided and there is evidence in later transactions of the sheer scale of the estate which must have required considerable administration.

Thomas, however, also found time to serve the Archbishop of Canterbury as his father had done before him. The Archbishop at that time was Henry Chichele who along with Henry VI co-founded All Soul’s College, Oxford (or The Warden and the College of the Souls of all Faithful People deceased in the University of Oxford to give it its full title) in 1438. Thomas Ballard donated a valuable covered mazer of Maplewood and gilt and he and Phillipe contributed funds to the building of the College as well. Mazers were highly prized drinking vessels & Thomas' is one of only 63 medieval examples that now survive and very few of them still retain their cover mounts as Thomas' does. These drinking vessels are shallow bowls without handles, with a broad flat foot and a knob or boss in the centre of the inside, known technically as the print. Thomas had his arms enamelled on the print. The mazer is on loan to the Ashmolean Museum as part of their silver collection.

As we know from the College of Arms there are two blazons relating to a Thomas Ballard at All Souls, Oxford (1448) and the British & Ashmolean Museum seals (15th Century) respectively of Sable a griffin regardant ermine and a Griffin sergeant with the crest of a demi griffin and it seems probable that they both refer to this Thomas although why one is a forward facing griffin (sergeant) and the other backwards (regardant) is unknown.

In return for his services to Archbishop Chichele he was rewarded with the office of Chief Keeper and Huntsman to the Archbishop of Canterbury. This was granted for life together with the custody of the Archbishop's parks, warrens, chases and woods within the counties of Kent, Surrey, Sussex and Middlesex on 8th March 1443. In an age in which hunting was essentially the only sport as well as a ready source of food this was a considerable gift and would have allowed Thomas the opportunity to entertain Knights & even Barons on an equal footing. Certainly he was trusted and befriended by them for we know that he acted as executor to Sir William Septvans in 1447.

We should not, however, think that Thomas was always a pillar of the Establishment because we know that he was granted a general pardon on 7th July 1450 by Henry VI at the Queen's request for his part in Jack Cade's rebellion. It is interesting to note that his son Humphrey Thomas in 1499 bequeathed a one third share of the residue of his estate to Thomas Cade, yeoman, surely not a coincidence. It is also intriguing to see that Richard Ballard of West Firle in Sussex and William Ballard & Agnes his wife and another William Ballard, possibly their son, were granted pardons for their part in the rebellion and the will of Thomas's father Gregory contains bequests to both a Richard Ballard, his servant, and a William Ballard.

Despite his involvement in the rebellion Thomas was still appointed to the office of Sheriff of Kent and keeper of Canterbury castle on 8th November 1452 in succession to Robert Hoorn. It became the practice to include the arms of the Sherriff’s of Kent on bosses on the ceiling of the cloisters at Canterbruy Cathedral and even today Thomas’ may be seen there blazoned as “Sable a Griffin segreant ermine, beaked and legged gule”. We are told by Hasted that Thomas held his shrievalty at Horton which, being only a few miles from Canterbury, was obviously the better placed of his manors from which to undertake the role of keeper of Canterbury castle.

Philippa’s father, Thomas Walsingham, died in 1456 and he bequeathed to Thomas Ballard “the Moroc Bible that my Lord Cardinal gave me written in the Boleyn hand” and also a “fine cup”. He bequeathed to his daughter Philippe broadcloth and other fine napery (linen used for household purposes).

One seemingly insignificant act that is recorded for Thomas is that he was granted a Commission of Array on 28th January 1460. The Commissions of array had been developed from the obligation of all free men to defend their country and Commissioners were usually experienced soldiers. Thomas was also Commissioned to levy and collect tax in Kent amounting to 293 pounds 7 shillings and 8 pence halfpenny on 1st July 1463 and this appears to be his last public service. Thomas recorded his will at the Manor of Horton on 20th April 1465 and he died on 18th August 1465 as we know from his Inquisition Port Mortem held at Rochester, Kent on 4th April 1466.

zh.Buckinghamshire, England, UK: 英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾
zh.France, Vila Real, Portugal: 葡萄牙比拉 雷亞爾福朗斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Ballard於1394年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾時，他的父親Gregory Ballard24歲，母親Catherine Gregory29歲。他和Philipe Walshingham至少有1個兒子。他去世於1465年8月18日在英國素可享年71歲。

nameMeanings.name: Ballard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ballard ‘bald-headed man’ (compare Bald 3).
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Balhard, composed of the elements bal, presumably meaning ‘torment, spitefulness’, and hard ‘hard, strong’. Compare Ballor .

variants.name: Ballon
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Sergio, Alfonso, Cesar, Conchita, Emigdio, Enrique, Felipe, Francisco, Jose Luis, Julio, Ruben.  Jewish Hyman, Isadore.

French and English (Middlesex): from the Old French personal name Ballon, Ballun, derived from the oblique stem of ancient Germanic Ballo, a short form of compound names based on the element bal, presumably meaning ‘torment, spitefulness’ (see Ballard ).

variants.name: Bald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Inge, Karl-Heinz, Konrad, Kurt, Otto.
German: from the ancient Germanic personal name Baldo, or a short form of various compound names such as Baldwin , formed with the first element bald ‘bold, strong’.

Scottish and English: from the Norman personal name Bald(e), Baud(e), occasionally Bold, a short form of the common ancient Germanic personal names Baldwin or Baldric, or possibly a survival of Old English Beald (compare Balding ); the relevant element in each of these is b(e)ald ‘brave’. As a hereditary surname it seems to have died out in England but survives in Scotland.

variants.name: Ballor
meanings: Altered form of French Ballard 2, reflecting the characteristic Canadian and American French rounding of the -ard ending. It is found mainly in MI.

variants.name: Bollard
meanings: English (Northamptonshire) and Irish: probably a variant of   Ballard  . According to MacLysaght, this is a surname of Dutch origin which was taken to Ireland early in the 18th century.
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Bolohard, composed of the elements bolo ‘friend, brother’ and hard ‘hard, strong’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Ballard was born in 1394, in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Gregory Ballard, was 24 and his mother, Catherine Gregory, was 29. He had at least 1 son with Philipe Walshingham. He died on 18 August 1465, in Sussex, England, at the age of 71.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Ballard est né en 1394, à Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Gregory Ballard, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Catherine Gregory, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Philipe Walshingham. Il est décédé le 18 août 1465, à Sussex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Ballard 1394 in Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gregory Ballard 24 und seine Mutter Catherine Gregory 29. Er hatte mit Philipe Walshingham mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 18. August 1465 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Sussex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Ballard è nato il nel 1394 nel Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Gregory Ballard, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Catherine Gregory, aveva 29 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Philipe Walshingham. Morì il 18 agosto 1465, nel Sussex, England, all'età di 71 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Ballard(이)가 1394년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gregory Ballard(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Catherine Gregory(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Philipe Walshingham(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1465년 8월 18일 영국 수세스에서 71세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Ballard nasceu em 1394, em Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Gregory Ballard, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Catherine Gregory, tinha 29 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Philipe Walshingham. Ele faleceu em 18 de agosto de 1465, em Sussex, Inglaterra, com 71 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Ballard родился в 1394 г. в местности буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Gregory Ballard было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Catherine Gregory, 29. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Philipe Walshingham. Он умер 18 августа 1465 г. в местности суссекс, Англия в возрасте 71 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Ballard nació en 1394, en Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Gregory Ballard, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Catherine Gregory, tenía 29 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Philipe Walshingham. Murió el 18 de agosto de 1465, en Sussex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años.
